Wild turkeys are increasingly common in suburban and urban areas, where they can block traffic, damage parked cars, or become aggressive toward residents.
While some residents view these urban birds as "pets," officials warn that turkeys can scratch vehicles or attack citizens, making them a significant nuisance.
To prevent conflicts, authorities advise never feeding turkeys, removing bird feeders, and using visual deterrents to keep them away from residential properties.
